item.title: Memmingen Travel Guide | Medieval City 1.5 Hours from Munich (2025)
item.description: Memmingen is one of Bavaria’s least-known but best-preserved medieval towns. Near-complete city walls, Marktplatz, half-timbered buildings — a small but complete open-air museum. 1.5 hours from Munich with Deutschlandticket.

item.title: Christmas and New Year in Germany | Munich Christmas Markets & Silvester (2025)
item.description: Christmas in Germany is very different from what you might expect. Weihnachten is quiet and family-focused; Silvester is surprisingly loud. From Munich’s Christmas markets to last year’s Silvester experience — German winter culture through expat eyes.

item.title: Deutschlandticket Day Trips from Munich | Bavaria with Kids (2025)
item.description: For 49 EUR per month you can take all local and regional trains across Germany. From Munich to Füssen, Garmisch, Salzburg, Starnberg Lake — routes we tested with two kids aged 1 and 5 and a double stroller.

item.title: Renting an Apartment in Germany | The Reality of Finding Housing in Munich (2025)
item.description: Finding a rental in Munich is one of Germany’s most difficult bureaucratic processes. Schufa score, employment contract, reference letters and hundreds of applications — it gets even harder for foreigners. First-hand experience.

item.title: Can You Drive in Germany with a Bulgarian License? | Turkish→Bulgarian Transfer Reality (2026)
item.description: I obtained Bulgarian citizenship and converted my Turkish license to a Bulgarian one. Sofia KAT centers, mandatory interpreter, 30-day wait and then the clear answer from Germany: no, it doesn’t work. The EU directive doesn’t allow it.

item.title: Driving License Exchange in Germany | The Reality of Turkish & Bulgarian Licenses
item.description: Failing to plan the driving license exchange process early after moving to Germany can be costly. My experience trying to exchange a license via Bulgaria — and why it failed.

item.title: How to Find Cheap Flights | Pegasus, Google Flights and Real Tactics
item.description: Finding cheap flights is not a matter of luck — it’s about knowing the system. How to catch great deals with Pegasus campaign hours, Google Flights alerts and flexible route planning.

item.title: How to Get a Schengen Visa (2026) | Kozmos, Appointments and Real Experiences
item.description: Getting a Schengen visa after the pandemic has gotten harder. Greece visa via Kozmos, appointment-finding tactics and application as a relative of an EU citizen — summary of 5 successful applications.

item.title: Georgia Travel Guide: Tbilisi & Batumi | 5-Day Real Experience (2026)
item.description: A missed flight from Istanbul to Georgia, a land border crossing, pushing a stroller through Tbilisi and a taxi argument in Batumi. An unembellished, real 5-day Georgia experience.

item.title: Hidden Gems in Tbilisi and Batumi | 15 Places You Haven't Seen
item.description: Every Georgia travel guide tells you the same places. This article has a different Georgia: lesser-known neighborhoods, hidden cafés, local experiences and corners not shown to standard tourists.

item.title: Greece Travel Guide | Athens, Islands and Practical Tips (2026)
item.description: Greece is the address of both history and sunshine. Acropolis in Athens, turquoise sea on the islands. Where to start in Greece, which requires a Schengen visa, and how to plan a budget?

item.title: Switzerland Basel Travel Guide | One City, Three Countries: Switzerland, France, Germany (2026)
item.description: In Basel you can have breakfast in France in the morning and lunch in Germany. This city, standing at the exact intersection point of Switzerland, France and Germany, means three countries in a single trip.

item.title: Switzerland Zurich Travel Guide | Things to Do, Transport & Budget (2026)
item.description: Zurich, Switzerland’s financial capital, is one of the world’s most expensive cities. But with the right planning, it’s possible to see the city’s best places without burning through your wallet.

item.title: Bosnia Herzegovina Sarajevo Travel Guide | Visa-Free, Budget & Historic (2026)
item.description: Sarajevo, with its Ottoman traces, war history and familiar faces, is a destination that is both close and deeply moving for travelers. Visa-free, affordable and just 1.5 hours by plane.

item.title: Kosovo Travel Guide | Pristina and Prizren | Visa-Free Balkan Route (2026)
item.description: Kosovo is one of the least explored countries in the Balkans. Visa-free, affordable and full of Ottoman traces. Pristina’s modern face and Prizren’s historic texture — two different experiences in one trip.

item.title: Skopje Travel Guide | North Macedonia Capital | Visa-Free (2026)
item.description: Skopje is one of the Balkans’ strangest and most interesting capitals. With giant statues, the Ottoman bazaar and the controversial Skopje 2014 architectural project, the city is like an open-air museum.

item.title: About
item.description:
Hi — I’m Barash
#

I live in Munich. I’m a Network Security Engineer. I’m a father of two — aged 1 and 5. And I own a double stroller whose dimensions I now know are incompatible with approximately 60% of European cobblestone streets.

I was born in Turkey, obtained Bulgarian citizenship, and moved to Germany. I’ve personally navigated the bureaucratic maze between these three countries — and I’m still navigating it.

item.title: Contact
item.description:
Get in Touch
#

I read and respond to every message.

📫 Email: info@share-hub-eu.online

When Can You Reach Out?
#

To share your experience If you’ve gone through a similar process in Germany or Europe — with different results or different experiences — I’d love to hear from you. This blog consists of one person’s notes, but reader experiences make these posts much stronger.
